architecture.summary: NICO is a neuro-inspired companion robot that integrates neural network modules for perception and reasoning with multiple explanation modalities (non-verbal behavioral cues, verbal explanations of intentions/knowledge/reasoning, and multimodal visualizations). The system generates explanations by extracting interpretable outputs from its neural components to communicate with non-expert users in human-robot interaction scenarios.
trained_components: neural network modules for perception and reasoning

claims.statement: Different types of explanations enhance the interaction experience in human-robot interaction
Non-verbal cues provide simple and intuitive feedback of robot inner states
Comprehensive verbal explanations communicate the robot's intentions, knowledge and reasoning
Multimodal explanations using visualizations, speech and text enhance explainability in HRI
Two novel XHRI approaches extract suitable verbal and multimodal explanations from neural network modules in HRI scenarios
